title: BBC visits aftermath of Israeli strike on Lebanon that killed family as IDF targets Hezbollah
description: A relative tells BBC those killed were civilians and not Hezbollah operatives, but the Israel Defence Forces says it was targeting "terrorist infrastructure".

title: Three more Iran football team members change minds over asylum
description: Four of seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

title: 'Gruesome' war bets fuel calls for crackdown on prediction markets
description: Predictions markets have hosted millions of dollars of bets related to the war in Iran.

title: Hamas urges key ally Iran to halt attacks on Gulf states
description: The Palestinian group also affirms Tehran's right to defend itself from "aggression" by the US and Israel.

title: Cuban protesters ransack Communist office as energy crisis deepens
description: Discontent is mounting over rolling blackouts and shortages of food, fuel and medicine, exacerbated by a US blockade.

title: Iraq taking private plane to Mexico for World Cup play-off
description: Iraq will travel to Mexico by private plane for their World Cup play-off despite calls from coach Graham Arnold to postpone the match amid the escalating US-Israeli war with Iran, the country's FA president Adnan Dirjal says.

title: Treasure hunter freed from prison after refusing to turn over shipwreck gold
description: Tommy Thompson spent more than a decade in prison after refusing to disclose the whereabouts of 500 missing gold coins.

title: Russia exploiting Iran war, Zelensky says after fresh bombardment
description: Volodymyr Zelensky cast concern over the supply of air-defence missiles after a heavy overnight attack kills at least five.

title: Rescuers blame weather and 'underprepared skiers' for rise in Alps avalanche deaths
description: The BBC joins a French Alps rescue team as the number of skiers killed this season passes 100.

title: Explosion at Amsterdam Jewish school 'a deliberate attack' says mayor
description: Security had been increased at Jewish public institutions following an earlier incident in Rotterdam.

title: Why the Chicago Bears could be moving to Indiana
description: While Illinois is trying to keep the team in Chicago's suburbs, Indiana lawmakers are offering a plan to finance a new stadium<br><br>

title: Pentagon tightens controls over Stars and Stripes after calling it "woke"
description: The new rules for the independent military newspaper are the Defense Department's latest effort to put extraordinary restrictions on journalists covering the agency.

title: Russian strike on Kyiv region kills 4 and wounds 15, with peace talks stalled
description: The strikes comes after the United States paused ceasefire talks between Russia and Ukraine due to the war with Iran.

title: In South Carolina, measles shows how far apart neighbors can be on vaccines
description: In South Carolina, some parents embrace vaccines, others opt out. Why do people make such different choices? A mix of politics, distrust and misinformation is pushing neighbors apart.

title: Brazil's ex-President Bolsonaro is in intensive care with pneumonia, hospital says
description: One of Bolsonaro's doctors described the former Brazilian president's medical condition as "serious."

title: Opinion: An ancient, sophisticated palate
description: Researchers looking at foodcrusts on the pottery shards of ancient humans say there's evidence of a wide variety of ingredients, indicating that they may have been experimenting with "recipes."

title: Why women have an especially tough time in Senegal's prisons
description: Women charged with a crime in Senegal are at the mercy of a slow judicial process and prisons that may lack basic supplies. They also face stigma that robs them of familial and community support.

title: With boom in prediction markets, some lawmakers worry about how to police themselves
description: House and Senate ethics committees give no financial disclosure guidance on event contracts or prediction markets — unlike stock, cryptocurrency and bond trades.

title: U.S. Embassy in Baghdad again urges Americans to leave Iraq as Trump touts strikes on Iran
description: In a post on Truth Social, President Trump claimed the U.S.  had "destroyed 100% of Iran's Military capability." The U.S. Embassy in Baghdad, meanwhile, urged Americans to "leave Iraq immediately."

title: Reframing Georgia O'Keeffe's legacy and protecting the land she loved
description: Georgia O'Keeffe called the New Mexico high desert "my country," but Pueblo peoples predated her. A more complex view is emerging amid efforts to preserve the land.

title: Iran war live: Trump urges allies to keep Hormuz open amid Gulf attacks
description: US President Donald Trump says allies who rely on Gulf oil must help keep the Strait of Hormuz open.

title: Pakistan strikes Afghan base after its president warns ‘red line’ crossed
description: Islamabad hits Kandahar facility after Taliban drones strike civilian areas and military sites as conflict intensifies.

title: Brazil’s jailed ex-president Bolsonaro hospitalised with lung infection
description: Far-right leader, whose persistent health woes are linked to 2018 stabbing, endorsed son to run in this year's election.

title: Bahrain and Saudi Arabia F1 race cancellations confirmed due to Iran war
description: Bahrain and Saudi Arabia's Formula One Grands Prix races will not be held in April on safety grounds due to the war.

title: Man City held at West Ham to lose ground on Premier League leaders Arsenal
description: Manchester City draw 1-1 with relegation-threatened West Ham to fall nine points behind Arsenal in Premier League race.

title: Trinidad and Tobago extends state of emergency for another three months
description: The Caribbean nation has spent 10 of the last 14 months under an emergency status that curtails key civil liberties.

title: Real Madrid beat Elche to pressure Barcelona for La Liga title
description: Federico Valverde scores his fifth goal in three games as Real Madrid beat Elche 4-1 to move one point behind Barcelona.

title: US-Israeli strike kills 15 at Isfahan factory, Iranian media says
description: Iranian media report the deaths in central Iran as Tehran launches new missile salvoes at Israeli targets.

title: Thousands in Madrid protest ‘forgotten’ Gaza, warn Iran war may spiral into
description: Thousands rallied in central Madrid calling for an end to the war involving the United States, Israel and Iran.

title: Hundreds protest against US-Israeli strikes on Iran in Seoul
description: Hundreds of peace activists and demonstrators marched in central Seoul on Saturday in protest of the US-Israeli strikes.
